key_evidence: In-window: xAI's own product Grok used to generate 7,000+ CSAM images of a single child victim (Techdirt, 2026-07-21); the class action against xAI was amended in early July 2026 to add minor plaintiffs.
In-window: on 2026-07-16 xAI sued its own user rather than remediating, invoking a terms-of-service indemnification clause to shift liability for victims onto the user (PetaPixel; Techdirt).

xAI's stated safety response is reactive and legally-mandated only: account suspensions and NCMEC reports (federally required), 52,222 suspensions / 73,604 NCMEC reports in 2026 (PetaPixel, 2026-07-16).
Child-safety harm is SUBSTANTIATED in-window; the May finding is not stale. xAI is already at the index floor (0) and a score cannot go below 0.

evidence.quote: Using Grok, the stepfather created more than 7,000 CSAM-related images of her. He also shared and traded the images with others on social media platforms.
evidence.claim: Grok, xAI's own product, was used to generate more than 7,000 CSAM images of one child victim.

evidence.quote: xAI...has now directly sued one of its own users, Terry Harwood, for creating CSAM with Grok, arguing that the indemnification clause in the terms of service means that the court should order Harwood to pay.
evidence.claim: Rather than remediate, xAI sued its own user and sought to shift victim-compensation liability via an indemnification clause.

evidence.quote: Defendant's actions were a calculated scheme to weaponize Plaintiff's tool for criminal ends, exposing real victims to profound and lasting harm.
evidence.claim: xAI's complaint frames the harm to real child victims as profound and lasting.

evidence.quote: xAI claims to have suspended 52,222 accounts and made 73,604 reports to the NCMEC in 2026, which has purportedly led to at least 244 arrests.
evidence.claim: xAI's safety response is limited to legally-mandated NCMEC reporting and account suspensions after harm occurs.

calibration_note: xAI/Grok is already at the absolute index floor (composite 0, harm-flag). The May 2026 proposal of 11.7 would have RAISED the score; that is inconsistent both with a floor entity and with fresh in-window evidence of continuing child-safety harm. The honest outcome is a CONFIRM at 0. A score cannot go below 0, so ongoing harm cannot be expressed as a further numeric drop; it is recorded as sustained floor placement with high confidence.
